text: T_C: For coupled Burgers-swept-KdV with smoothed-bore u IC (u_L=1.5, u_R=0) and KdV soliton v IC (A=1.5 sech^2(x+8)), does the soliton survive the encounter with the bore (transmit / reflect / fuse / destroyed)? Compute u(x,t),v(x,t) on [-15,15], periodic, Nx=256, T=8.0, with final v peak amplitude >=0.5 and |u_max|<5.

method: Fourier pseudospectral (NO dealiasing) + classical RK4; Nx=256, L=30, dt=5e-4.
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity baseline; bank confirms FPS+RK4 is canonical bare baseline.

rationale: Immediate spectral overflow within 6 RK4 steps from undealiased v^2 + sharp bore in u producing alias-folded high-k modes amplified by v_xxx (k_max^3 ~ 1230).
Single-component upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing per BKdV-S1-r1.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product + RK4.
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S1-r1 confirmed 2/3 dealias isolates aliasing from v_xxx stiffness; canonical single-knob upgrade.

rationale: Stable, mass-conserving, u_max<5 OK; but v_peak=0.4733 misses target 0.5, and u_min growing increasingly negative shows Gibbs ringing from the un-dissipated bore. The bore in u contaminates v via -d_x(uv) coupling, dragging the soliton peak below threshold.

Single-component upgrade: add linear u-viscosity nu*u_xx with nu=5e-2 (dispatcher-cited BKdV-S6 prescription for bore-like u IC). Diffusion CFL: nu*k_max^2*dt = 5e-2*718*5e-4 = 0.018 << RK4 bound, so dt can stay at 5e-4. v-equation unchanged.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + RK4 + linear u-viscosity nu*u_xx (nu=5e-2) in u-equation only; periodic [-15,15], Nx=256.
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S1-r1/r2 confirm dealias+RK4 stability; BKdV-S7-r2 confirms the BKdV physical breakdown (v_max drop, u_max climb) is real; the bore-Gibbs failure mode in F2 is the kind targeted by BKdV-S6 nu_linear=5e-2 prescription (per dispatcher) — one extra explicit linear term, smallest meaningful escalation vs E2.

rationale: All three phenomenon target conditions met: (1) final v_peak=0.5019 >= 0.5 (PASS), (2) |u_max|=1.43 < 5 (PASS, with large margin), (3) bore has not blown up (smoothly propagating, u in [0.28, 1.43] at T=8). Soliton clearly transmitted through the bore: initial x=-8 -> final peak x=10.78 (traveled ~18.8 in T=8, mean speed 2.35 — slower than free soliton speed 3.0 because of interaction-induced slowdown). v_peak history shows refraction signature: 1.5 -> 0.81 (entering bore region by t=1) -> 0.56 (passing through by t=2) -> oscillating ~0.42-0.50 after (post-interaction radiation). u-viscosity smoothly damped Gibbs (u_min went from -2.55 in E2 to +0.28 in E3 by T=8). Phenomenon: TRANSMISSION with significant amplitude loss (~67%) but soliton survives as a recognizable peak. Mass conservation is essentially machine precision.
E3 meets all phenomenon target criteria with positive self-assessment. Iteration cap of 3 reached. candidate.py reflects E3 (final solver).
